terms.definition: A compact representation of classified structure and knowledge state. It is never a score.
terms.scope_note: Encodes structure and knowledge state only.

terms.definition: The disclosed path by which a fund obtains its strategy exposure—not a guess from a single holding or instrument.
terms.scope_note: Separates permitted instruments from the evidenced governing route.

terms.definition: The disclosed lookback or time horizon governing a signal—not its evaluation or rebalance frequency.
terms.scope_note: Evaluation and rebalance cadence are not substitutes.

terms.definition: A methodology seeking to reproduce material exposures or pre-fee behavior of a representative managed-futures set without implying index tracking.
terms.scope_note: Replication and index tracking are separate governed claims.

terms.definition: The governed relationship between a fund and an index or comparison benchmark, kept separate from replication language.
terms.scope_note: A named comparison benchmark does not establish tracking.
